en.extract:
The crested heleia (Heleia dohertyi), also known as the crested ibon or crested white-eye, is a species of bird in the white-eye family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to the Lesser Sunda Islands (Sumbawa and Flores).
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

The '''crested heleia''' ('''''Heleia dohertyi'''''), also known as the '''crested ibon''' or '''crested white-eye''',<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/ioc-lists/master-list-2/ |title=Master Lists: v15.1 |website=[[IOC World Bird List]] |publisher=[[International Ornithologists' Union]] |access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the white-eye family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Lesser Sunda Islands]] ([[Sumbawa]] and [[Flores]]).

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].
